name: Gazzew Boba U4T
description: Full-sound tactile counterpart to the Boba U4 — same nylon housing, same pronounced round bump near the top of the stroke, but without the foam dampeners, so the bottom-out and top-out ring freely. The result is a tactile with a deep, full-bodied thud on each keypress. Popular for typists who want a strong round bump without sacrificing acoustic character. Pre-lubed on the rails; spring-rattling on the upstroke is common and addressed with a thin oil on the spring.
